top_findings: bin/stampede.sh defaults metaswarm commanders to claude-haiku-4.5 unless overridden.
Launcher does not enforce premium/banned model selection for commander launches.
Metaswarm collaboration bus creation and commander-failure recording are present.
Commander contract lacks explicit stop/teardown/recovery handling.
launch_requested event shape and banned-model detection mechanism are unspecified.
Depth Guard and premium/banned model lists are otherwise internally consistent.
skills/SKILL.md lacks explicit commander/metaswarm launch phrasing and premium-policy enforcement.
Structured live telemetry is not emitted from the skill.
Lifecycle phase token stampedeing is inconsistent.
README and AGENTS disagree on branch naming.
Reviewed docs do not describe commander launch profile, telemetry, or stop/recovery behavior.
README and AGENTS conflict on queue path defaults.
Preflight does not positively verify actual model selection, so silent downgrade may escape detection.

blockers: The task tool returned synchronous results without a distinct background agent id; launch_started events used worker ids as the agent_id placeholder.
swarm_progress.py uses a shared .tmp-swarm-state.json path, so concurrent updates collided during the run; completion events required retries.
consensus_notes: Premium-only policy is documented but not strongly enforced at launcher/skill boundaries.
Silent downgrade detection and negative-test coverage are insufficient.
Stop/teardown/recovery expectations are under-specified in commander-facing docs and prompts.
Metaswarm collaboration bus creation and some failure recording hooks are present.
conflict_notes: Documentation conflicts on branch naming and queue path defaults.
Commander contract wording around Depth Guard leaves ambiguity between squad-lead and worker constraints.
